issues.description: Illustrative arithmetic correctly applying the 10% rule from the reference; explicitly permitted as derived/illustrative. Does not contradict reference.

issues.claim_in_outline: Lions, sharks, eagles, apex predators rare; grain vs. beef feeds more people
issues.description: Standard illustrative organisms/examples permitted by instructions; consistent with the 10% rule and food-chain length in the reference. Not presented as cited facts contradicting the reference.

issues.claim_in_outline: ASSIMILATION: plants take up NO3-/NH4+ to build amino acids and nucleic acids; animals get N by eating.
issues.description: Reference defines assimilation only as 'plants take up NO3-/NH4+.' The added detail (amino acids/nucleic acids, animals eating) is standard textbook elaboration, not stated in the reference, but does not contradict it. Acceptable as derived/illustrative.
issues.suggested_fix: Optional: flag the molecular detail as illustrative; no contradiction with reference.

issues.claim_in_outline: Phytoplankton high turnover/productivity per unit biomass means they supply MORE energy over time; energy pyramid never inverts even when biomass does.
issues.description: Reference states biomass pyramids can invert in aquatic systems (phytoplankton support more zooplankton biomass at a moment) and energy pyramid is always upright. The turnover/productivity reasoning is a faithful explanatory expansion, fully consistent with reference. No new unsupported factual claim.

issues.claim_in_outline: Gulf of Mexico dead zone, biomagnification, Module 15 preview
issues.description: Gulf of Mexico dead zone appears in reference. Module 15 framing and biomagnification are forward-looking course-structure references, not factual claims contradicting the reference.

overall_assessment: Revision (round 2) introduced no new unsupported factual claims. All core data — 10% rule, GPP/NPP, biome NPP rankings, pyramid behaviors, 280→>420 ppm (2024), CO2+H2O→H2CO3 acidification, the five nitrogen steps and their microbes (Rhizobium, cyanobacteria, Nitrosomonas, Nitrobacter, Haber-Bosch), and eutrophication/Gulf of Mexico — match the reference. Illustrative arithmetic and standard illustrative organisms (lions, sharks, eagles, grain-vs-beef, one-tree-many-insects) are framed as examples/derivations, not as cited facts, and none contradict the reference. Two minor elaborations (assimilation into amino acids/nucleic acids; phytoplankton turnover reasoning) go slightly beyond the reference wording but are consistent and acceptable as derived/illustrative. Verdict: pass.
